insights.claim: The population-weighted U.S. average for short sleep is 36.54%.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted mean across ZCTAs with estimates.

insights.claim: The most-deprived ADI tenth averages 9.0 points higher than the least-deprived tenth.
insights.method_note: Population-weighted ADI national-rank decile means; ecological, not causal.

insights.claim: Across ZCTAs, short sleep is most associated with college graduates (Spearman rho=-0.526, n=32,221).
insights.method_note: Spearman rank correlation; place-level association only.
Maximum ZCTA value; ties broken by population.

insights.claim: About 39.1 million people live in ZCTAs at or above the high-burden threshold (41.8%).
insights.method_note: Population in ZCTAs at or above the 90th percentile (41.8%).
